papers.title_en: Detached Skip-Links and $R$-Probe: Decoupling Feature Aggregation from Gradient Propagation for MLLM OCR
papers.authors: Ziye Yuan, Ruchang Yao, Chengxin Zheng, Yusheng Zhao, Daxiang Dong, Ming Zhang
papers.evidence: 优化多模态大模型在OCR任务中的特征融合
papers.tldr: 针对多模态大模型在OCR任务中细粒度视觉细节丢失的问题，本文发现传统的跳跃连接会导致高层语义梯度干扰底层视觉信号。为此，作者提出了“分离式跳跃连接”（Detached Skip-Links），在保留前向特征融合的同时阻断反向梯度传播，并引入R-Probe评估视觉特征的可重建性。该方法在不增加参数的情况下，显著提升了模型在OCR及通用多模态任务上的表现。
papers.abstract_en: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) excel at high-level reasoning yet fail on OCR tasks where fine-grained visual details are compromised or misaligned. We identify an overlooked optimization issue in multi-layer feature fusion. Skip pathways introduce direct back-propagation paths from high-level semantic objectives to early visual layers. This mechanism overwrites low-level signals and destabilizes training. To mitigate this gradient interference, we propose Detached Skip-Links, a minimal modification that reuses shallow features in the forward pass while stopping gradients through the skip branch during joint training. This asymmetric design reduces gradient interference, improving stability and convergence without adding learnable parameters. To diagnose whether fine-grained information is preserved and usable by an LLM, we introduce $R$-Probe, which measures pixel-level reconstructability of projected visual tokens using a shallow decoder initialized from the first quarter of the LLM layers. Across multiple ViT backbones and multimodal benchmarks, and at scales up to 7M training samples, our approach consistently improves OCR-centric benchmarks and delivers clear gains on general multimodal tasks.

papers.title_en: SLARM: Streaming and Language-Aligned Reconstruction Model for Dynamic Scenes
papers.authors: Zhicheng Qiu, Jiarui Meng, Tong-an Luo, Yican Huang, Xuan Feng, Xuanfu Li, ZHan Xu
papers.evidence: 语言对齐的重建与语义查询
papers.tldr: SLARM是一个统一的动态场景重建、语义理解和实时流式推理的前馈模型。它通过高阶运动建模捕捉复杂非均匀运动，并蒸馏LSeg语义特征实现语言对齐，支持自然语言查询。采用窗口因果注意力机制实现低延迟流式处理，在动态估计、渲染质量和场景解析方面均达到SOTA水平，显著提升了运动精度和重建效果。
papers.abstract_en: We propose SLARM, a feed-forward model that unifies dynamic scene reconstruction, semantic understanding, and real-time streaming inference. SLARM captures complex, non-uniform motion through higher-order motion modeling, trained solely on differentiable renderings without any flow supervision. Besides, SLARM distills semantic features from LSeg to obtain language-aligned representations. This design enables semantic querying via natural language, and the tight coupling between semantics and geometry further enhances the accuracy and robustness of dynamic reconstruction. Moreover, SLARM processes image sequences using window-based causal attention, achieving stable, low-latency streaming inference without accumulating memory cost. Within this unified framework, SLARM achieves state-of-the-art results in dynamic estimation, rendering quality, and scene parsing, improving motion accuracy by 21%, reconstruction PSNR by 1.6 dB, and segmentation mIoU by 20% over existing methods.

papers.title_en: Agile-VLA: Few-Shot Industrial Pose Rectification via Implicit Affordance Anchoring
papers.authors: Teng Yan, Zhengyang Pei, Chengyu Shi, Yue Yu, Yikun Chen, Zilong Zhu, Zelin Fang, Kaile Guo, Zihang Wang, Peigen Tian, Bingzhuo Zhong
papers.evidence: 用于工业任务的视觉-语言-动作模型
papers.tldr: Agile-VLA 针对边缘设备上视觉语言动作（VLA）模型推理延迟高与控制频率要求高的矛盾，提出了一种分层框架。通过隐式可操作性锚定机制，将几何视觉线索映射为动作原语，并采用异步双流架构解耦感知与控制。该系统仅需5次演示即可实现复杂工件的姿态纠正，显著提升了工业机器人在资源受限环境下的作业效率。
papers.abstract_en: Deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models on resource-constrained edge platforms encounters a fundamental conflict between high-latency semantic inference and the high-frequency control required for dynamic manipulation. To address the challenge, this paper presents Agile-VLA, a hierarchical framework designed for industrial pose reorientation tasks on edge devices such as the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. The core innovation is an Implicit Affordance Anchoring mechanism that directly maps geometric visual cues, specifically centroid and rim keypoint anchors, into structured parametric action primitives, thereby substantially reducing reliance on high-latency semantic inference during closed-loop control. By decoupling perception (10 Hz) from control (50 Hz) via an asynchronous dual-stream architecture, the system effectively mitigates the frequency mismatch inherent in edge-based robot learning. Experimental results on a standard 6-DoF manipulator demonstrate that Agile-VLA achieves robust rectification of complex, irregular workpieces using only 5-shot demonstrations through extrinsic dexterity.

papers.title_en: A Multimodal Framework for Human-Multi-Agent Interaction
papers.authors: Shaid Hasan, Breenice Lee, Sujan Sarker, Tariq Iqbal
papers.evidence: 具有 LLM 驱动规划的多模态人机交互框架
papers.tldr: 针对现有系统在多机器人社交环境中难以整合多模态感知与协调决策的问题，本文提出了一种人与多智能体交互的框架。该框架赋予每个机器人基于大语言模型（LLM）的认知与规划能力，并结合中心化协调机制管理轮替与参与，避免冲突。通过在双人型机器人上的实验，展示了该系统在语音、手势、注视和移动方面的协同表现，为自然且可扩展的物理空间交互提供了新方案。
papers.abstract_en: Human-robot interaction is increasingly moving toward multi-robot, socially grounded environments. Existing systems struggle to integrate multimodal perception, embodied expression, and coordinated decision-making in a unified framework. This limits natural and scalable interaction in shared physical spaces. We address this gap by introducing a multimodal framework for human-multi-agent interaction in which each robot operates as an autonomous cognitive agent with integrated multimodal perception and Large Language Model (LLM)-driven planning grounded in embodiment. At the team level, a centralized coordination mechanism regulates turn-taking and agent participation to prevent overlapping speech and conflicting actions. Implemented on two humanoid robots, our framework enables coherent multi-agent interaction through interaction policies that combine speech, gesture, gaze, and locomotion. Representative interaction runs demonstrate coordinated multimodal reasoning across agents and grounded embodied responses. Future work will focus on larger-scale user studies and deeper exploration of socially grounded multi-agent interaction dynamics.

papers.title_en: Beyond Attention Magnitude: Leveraging Inter-layer Rank Consistency for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.authors: Peiju Liu, Jinming Liu, Xipeng Qiu, Xuanjing Huang
papers.evidence: 用于机器人操作的高效视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型
papers.tldr: 针对视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型推理延迟高的问题，本文挑战了仅依赖注意力权重进行Token缩减的传统做法。提出TIES框架，利用跨层Token排名一致性动态选择关键视觉Token。该方法无需额外训练，在减少78% Token使用的同时，将机器人操作成功率提升了6%，实现了效率与性能的双重提升。
papers.abstract_en: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models excel in robotic manipulation but suffer from significant inference latency due to processing dense visual tokens. Existing token reduction methods predominantly rely on attention magnitude as a static selection. In this work, we challenge this assumption, revealing that high-attention tokens are task-dependent and can even degrade policy performance. To address this, we introduce \textbf{TIES} (\textbf{T}au-guided \textbf{I}nter-layer \textbf{E}fficient \textbf{S}election), a dynamic framework guided by inter-layer token ranking consistency. By adaptively balancing attention magnitude with ranking consistency, TIES ensures robust token selection without requiring additional training. On the CogACT + SIMPLER benchmark, TIES improves average success rates by 6\% while reducing token usage by 78\%, and demonstrate strong generalization across diverse decoders and benchmarks.

papers.title_en: Curriculum-Driven 3D CT Report Generation via Language-Free Visual Grafting and Zone-Constrained Compression
papers.authors: V. K. Cody Bumgardner, Mitchell A. Klusty, Mahmut S. Gokmen, Evan W. Damron
papers.evidence: 使用Llama 3.2解码器将输出定位在视觉特征中
papers.tldr: 针对3D CT影像报告生成中序列过长、类别不平衡及大模型忽视视觉信息的问题，本文提出Ker-VLJEPA-3B框架。该框架采用四阶段课程学习，利用无语言监督的自监督视觉编码器，通过区域约束交叉注意力将切片嵌入压缩为32个空间定位令牌，并结合PCA白化和正向发现策略。在CT-RATE基准测试中，该模型在宏F1指标上超越了现有SOTA，显著提升了报告生成的准确性与视觉关联度。
papers.abstract_en: Automated radiology report generation from 3D computed tomography (CT) volumes is challenging due to extreme sequence lengths, severe class imbalance, and the tendency of large language models (LLMs) to ignore visual tokens in favor of linguistic priors. We present Ker-VLJEPA-3B, a four-phase curriculum learning framework for free-text report generation from thoracic CT volumes. A phased training curriculum progressively adapts a Llama 3.2 3B decoder to ground its output in visual features from a frozen, self-supervised encoder. Our visual backbone (LeJEPA ViT-Large) is trained via self-supervised joint-embedding prediction on unlabeled CTs, without text supervision. Unlike contrastive models (CLIP, BiomedCLIP), this language-free backbone yields modality-pure representations. Vision-language alignment is deferred to the curriculum's bridge and generation phases. This modality-agnostic design can integrate any self-supervised encoder into an LLM without paired text during foundation training. Methodological innovations include: (1) zone-constrained cross-attention compressing slice embeddings into 32 spatially-grounded visual tokens; (2) PCA whitening of anisotropic LLM embeddings; (3) a positive-findings-only strategy eliminating posterior collapse; (4) warm bridge initialization transferring projection weights; and (5) selective cross-attention freezing with elastic weight consolidation to prevent catastrophic forgetting. Evaluated on the CT-RATE benchmark (2,984 validation volumes, 18 classes), Ker-VLJEPA-3B achieves a macro F1 of 0.429, surpassing the state-of-the-art (U-VLM, macro F1 = 0.414) by 3.6%, and reaching 0.448 (+8.2%) with threshold optimization. Ablation studies confirm 56.6% of generation quality derives from patient-specific visual content. Code and weights are available.
